text: When working with APIs, you often need to send HTTP requests to a server to get or send data. Most APIs use JSON format to exchange information, which is easy to read and process. First, you need to handle authentication, usually by including an API key or token in your request. Be careful with rate limits—many APIs restrict how many requests you can make in a certain time. If you exceed this limit, you may get an error. Good error handling is essential: always check the response status and manage problems like network issues or invalid data. With practice, these steps become straightforward.
